mission.title: netdata: Docs: Add instructions to persist metrics and restart policy
mission.description:
I've added two new instructions to the Docker docs:

1. Added a `--restart unless-stopped` policy to both the `docker run...` and Docker Compose methods of starting the container. (#8800)
2. Added a section containing instructions on how to create folders for the volumes, set permissions, and add the volumes to both `docker run...` and Docker Compose methods.

Now, I'm no Docker expert. These instructions worked for me, but if there's a way to optimi

steps.title: Verify the fix

resolution.summary:
##### Summary

This is a documentation only fix.

##### Additional Information

The `docker-compose.yml` equivalent of the docker command line example given in the file was missing another volume.
codeSnippets:
to the docker-compose
This is not good and i think we shouldnt suggest it.

And i think

https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/8813#issuecomment-618444252

this should be default and not in `if you want to persist` section.
> https://docs.docker.com/develop/dev-best-practices/#where-and-how-to-persist-application-data#where-and-how-to-persist-application-data

> Avoid storing application data in your container’s writable layer using storage drivers. This increases the size of your container and is less efficient from an I/O perspective than using volumes or bind mounts.
Instead, store data using volumes.

That is what we are doing now ^^


> One case where it is appropriate to use bind mounts is during development, when you may want to mount your source directory or a binary you just built into your container. For production, use a volume instead, mounting it into the same location as you mounted a bind mount during development.

Bind mounts for development (not a thing in our case), named volumes for production

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with netdata installed or the issue environment reproducible.
